content:
i found a video on the youtube teach how to write abstraction and drivers(https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3VU0hfsbHdc) it shows how to write drivers. However, maybe the version is different, i can't use something like: unsafe { bindings::mentor_write(addr, value) };
showed in the video to use the function. I then tried to use it in a simple wrapper. I created mentor.rs in the rust/kernel folder as follows
1718184640353.png
My rust driver module is as follows:
1718185117746.png
But when I compile, I get a Ld: error: undefined symbol:......
1718185168723.png
Do you have any solutions

in the video, there is no implementation, it just shows a header file like this:
1718188457583.png
I am also very confused about what he did in the video, does anyone know how he did it.

So, your extern "C" block is how you would do it if you were not using bindgen. What you need to do is to add the header file you shared in include/linux/mentor.h, and then you add that file to the list in rust/bindings/bindings_helper.h. Once you do that, crate::bindings::mentor_write will let you access the function.
However you will still get the same error, because the error means that mentor_read is not implemented anywhere. You need an actual implementation somewhere for that error to go away.

This is just one example given in the video that I want to use to learn how to securely wrap code generated by bindgen. But that seems a little difficult, I'm trying to implement rust support for the tty0tty module and rewrite it, and I want to first determine which functions or constructs I need to wrap securely. To do this, I intend to implement the module in an unwrapped version, as he shows, but the current r4l does not seem to support the approach in his diagram, how should I get to the state shown in his picture?
1718189957768.png
You need to include the header in rust/bindings/bindings_helper.h and that should be enough.

1718190538635.png
1718190568430.png
1718190611382.png
There seems to be something wrong, I can't seem to use these functions as shown in his diagram, what went wrong?
Adding the header to bindings_helper.h does not create a module in kernel. It just adds C functions to kernel::bindings::.

yes, my mentor.rs in the rust/kernel is just like this, and i add "pub mod mentor" to lib.rs. And it back to the original problem.
1718190925053.png
the mentor.rs is what the bindings_generated.rs new, after i  include the header in rust/bindings/bindings_helper.h

now, the problem is below, if i replace bindings:: with mentor:: it will back to the original "ld.lld" problem
1718191084623.png

image.png
i am not sure whether your mean is this.

Alice Ryhl said:

Did you define a kernel::mentor module in rust/kernel/lib.rs?

now, the problem is below, if i replace bindings:: with mentor:: it will back to the original "ld.lld" problem

Yes, and like I said previously, you get that error because you have no implementation of those functions.
You're calling functions that don't actually exist.
so, you mean my way to use the header file function generated from the bindgen is correct. It reach error is because the function I called does not exist. Right?
How about the struct in the header file? Can i use them just like the function way?
